title: wolftpm: implement Device type (init/cleanup/get_random/pcr_read)
description:
Implement the Device type wrapping WOLFTPM2_DEV with safe init, cleanup, and core operations.

## What
- Device struct wrapping WOLFTPM2_DEV (opaque, not Clone)
- Device::open() -> Result<Device> — opens /dev/tpm0 via wolfTPM2_Init with WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV
- Device::open_swtpm(addr: &str) -> Result<Device> — connects to swtpm/IBM simulator via TCP
- Device: Send (can move across threads), not Sync
- Drop impl: calls wolfTPM2_Cleanup
- get_random(n: usize) -> Result<Vec<u8>> — wolfTPM2_GetRandom
- pcr_read(index: u8) -> Result<[u8; 32]> — wolfTPM2_ReadPCR (SHA-256 bank)

## Files
- wolftpm/src/device.rs (new)
- wolftpm/src/lib.rs (add pub mod device; pub use device::Device)

## Notes
WOLFTPM2_DEV must be pinned or heap-allocated if it contains self-referential pointers — check wolfTPM source to confirm whether it is safe to move after init.
